evals.prompt: Design the UI for a project management dashboard. Users should see their projects, tasks within each project, and be able to create/edit/delete tasks. It's a React web app. We don't have a designer — I need to figure out the screens and flows myself.
evals.expected_output: Should map the user flow first (dashboard → project → task), then specify each screen with all states (loading, empty, error, loaded), define interaction patterns for CRUD operations, and include responsive behavior.
assertions: Maps the user flow before designing individual screens
Defines at least 3 screens (dashboard, project detail, task detail/modal)
Each screen has loading, empty, and error states defined
Empty state has a helpful CTA (not just 'no items')
CRUD interactions have specified feedback (toast, inline, redirect)
Responsive behavior defined for mobile vs desktop
Produces screen specifications using the template

evals.prompt: I need to design a multi-step checkout flow for an e-commerce app. Steps: cart review → shipping address → payment → confirmation. It needs to work on mobile too.
evals.expected_output: Should design the complete flow with all states including error recovery, define form validation patterns, address mobile-specific concerns, and handle edge cases.
assertions: Flow includes progress indicator showing current step
Each step saves data (user doesn't lose progress on back navigation)
Form validation strategy specified (when errors show, how they clear)
Payment step has loading state during processing
Error recovery for payment failure doesn't lose form data
Confirmation step shows order summary before final submit
Mobile layout addresses keyboard handling for address/payment forms
Handles edge case: cart modified during checkout, session timeout
